text: The cheetah sprints across the savanna faster than any other land animal.
A narwhal is a marine mammal whose tusk spirals out from its jaw.
The peregrine falcon dives toward its prey at tremendous speed.
An octopus uses its eight arms to crawl along the reef.
The grizzly bear forages for berries before its winter hibernation.
A chameleon shifts its skin color to blend with the branch.
The emperor penguin huddles with its colony against the Antarctic wind.
A honeybee carries pollen back from the meadow to the hive.
The gray wolf hunts in a coordinated pack across the tundra.
A komodo dragon ambushes the deer with a venomous bite.
The bottlenose dolphin echolocates fish in the murky bay.
An elephant uses its trunk to draw water from the river.
The red kangaroo bounds across the outback on powerful hind legs.
A jaguar drags its kill up into the canopy of the rainforest.
The arctic fox grows a white coat to hide in the snow.
The mass spectrometer ionizes the sample and sorts ions by mass.
A centrifuge spins the test tubes to separate the plasma from the cells.
The electron microscope resolves structures far smaller than a wavelength of light.
A pipette dispenses a precise microliter volume into the well plate.
The spectrophotometer measures how much light the solution absorbs.
An autoclave sterilizes the glassware with pressurized steam.
The oscilloscope traces the voltage waveform on its display.
A Bunsen burner heats the flask over a steady blue flame.
The chromatography column separates the mixture into its components.
A pH meter reports the acidity of the buffer solution.
The thermocycler runs the polymerase chain reaction across many temperature cycles.
An analytical balance weighs the reagent to the nearest milligram.
The fume hood vents toxic vapors away from the technician.
A microtome slices the tissue into ultra-thin sections for the slide.
The gradient ∇f points in the direction of steepest ascent of the scalar field.
A definite integral ∫ computes the area swept beneath the curve.
The summation ∑ accumulates each term of the infinite series.
A partial derivative ∂u/∂x measures change while the other variables stay fixed.
The constant π relates a circle’s circumference to its diameter.
The fine-structure constant α governs the strength of electromagnetic coupling.
A mass spectrum plots intensity against the mass-to-charge ratio m/z.
The inequality x ≤ y bounds the solution set from above.
The wavefunction ψ encodes the probability amplitude of the particle.
A photon of frequency ν carries energy proportional to Planck’s constant.
The Laplacian ∇²φ appears in the heat and the wave equations alike.
The molecule H₂O bends at an angle of about 104.5 degrees.
A vector field ⃗F can be decomposed into curl-free and divergence-free parts.
The eigenvalue λ satisfies the characteristic equation of the matrix.
Entropy ΔS never decreases for an isolated thermodynamic system.
The locomotive hauls a long freight train across the mountain pass.
A hydrofoil lifts its hull above the water as it accelerates.
The cargo ship ferries containers across the open ocean.
A helicopter hovers above the helipad before it descends.
The electric sedan recharges its battery at the roadside station.
A submarine dives beneath the thermocline to evade detection.
The bullet train glides along the maglev track at high speed.
A glider rides the thermal updraft without any engine.
The motorcycle leans into the curve along the coastal highway.
A tractor plows the field ahead of the spring planting.
The airliner taxis to the runway before its transatlantic flight.
A hot-air balloon drifts over the valley with the morning breeze.
The cable car climbs the steep slope toward the summit station.
A forklift stacks pallets in the warehouse aisle.
The redwood towers over the forest as one of the tallest living trees.
A sunflower turns its face to track the sun across the sky.
The Venus flytrap snaps shut on an insect that touches its hairs.
A fern unfurls its fronds in the damp shade of the understory.
The saguaro cactus stores water in its ribbed green trunk.
A bamboo shoot grows several centimeters in a single day.
The orchid anchors its roots to the bark of a rainforest tree.
A water lily floats its broad pads on the surface of the pond.
The oak drops thousands of acorns onto the woodland floor.
A moss carpets the boulder in a soft green layer.
The lavender attracts bees with its fragrant purple spikes.
A mangrove props itself above the tidal mud on stilted roots.
The wheat ripens to gold before the autumn harvest.
A pitcher plant traps insects in its slippery fluid-filled cup.
Jupiter is the largest planet and shelters dozens of moons.
A comet grows a glowing tail as it nears the inner solar system.
The Andromeda galaxy spirals toward our own Milky Way.
A neutron star packs more mass than the Sun into a city-sized sphere.
The red giant Betelgeuse swells near the end of its stellar life.
A pulsar sweeps its radio beam past Earth with clockwork regularity.
Saturn wears a bright system of icy rings around its equator.
A black hole bends light around its event horizon.
The asteroid Vesta orbits the Sun within the main belt.
A supernova outshines its entire host galaxy for a few weeks.
The dwarf planet Pluto drags a thin atmosphere through its long orbit.
A quasar blazes from the heart of a distant active galaxy.
The Moon raises ocean tides as it circles the Earth.
A nebula condenses cold gas into the first new stars.
The honeybee pollinates the sunflower while gathering its nectar.
The redwood shelters the gray wolf in the shade of its trunk.
The mass spectrometer measures the mass-to-charge ratio m/z of the sample.
The wavefunction ψ governs the behavior of the photon.
The narwhal hunts fish beneath the polar ice near the emperor penguin.
The submarine surfaces beside the cargo ship in the harbor.
The comet orbits Jupiter before it swings past Saturn.
The centrifuge spins the plasma that the spectrophotometer then analyzes.
The lavender attracts the honeybee away from the orchid.
The gradient ∇f drives the photon along the steepest path of the field.
The locomotive hauls the freight that the forklift loaded at the warehouse.
The Moon pulls the tide that lifts the cargo ship in the bay.
The cheetah chases the red kangaroo across the open plain.
The oak drops acorns that the grizzly bear gathers before winter.
The electron microscope resolves the tissue that the microtome sliced.
The integral ∫ sums the area that the summation ∑ approximates.
